project.name: Personal Website Redesign
project.goal: Restyle the existing personal website into a minimal editorial portfolio inspired by the provided reference image.
project.implementationRule: Ignore all current visual styles. Preserve and reuse existing components, routes, data, and functionality whenever they already exist.

principles: No decorative cards, gradients, shadows, or glass effects

typography.fontLoading: Use an existing locally configured monospace font if present. Otherwise load IBM Plex Mono.

emphasis.style: Use medium or semibold weight for important phrases rather than larger font sizes.
emphasis.heroHighlight: Apply weight 600 to one short phrase inside the hero statement.

layout.contentAlignment: Centered page container with all text left-aligned.

navigation.mobileBehavior: Keep navigation visible on one line when possible. Do not use a hamburger menu unless the current project already requires one.

home.eyebrow: new work / open to interesting work
home.introInstruction: Use the existing personal information, roles, interests, and project names already present in the website. Do not invent biographical claims.
home.supportingCopy: Use existing personal copy where available. Use lorem ipsum only where content is missing.
home.contactCta: End the supporting paragraph with a short underlined contact link such as “say hello.”

work.instruction: Reuse the existing work or experience component and data. Restyle it into a simple vertical text list without cards.

projects.instruction: Replace the reference site's writing section with projects. Reuse the existing projects component and existing project data.
itemStructure: repository and live links when already available
projects.visualStyle: Use a clean stacked list separated by subtle borders. Do not use image-heavy project cards unless the current project explicitly depends on them.

reusePolicy: Reuse existing Header, Navigation, Footer, About, Experience, Project, Contact, and layout components when present.
Change their markup only when necessary for accessibility or the new layout.
Do not create duplicate components that provide the same function.
Keep existing routing, link destinations, project data, analytics, forms, and integrations working.

interaction.transitions: color 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease, opacity 150ms ease

links.default: Underlined only for inline calls to action; navigation uses the active underline.

interaction.motion: No entrance animations, parallax, or large transforms. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.

rules: Allow the header to wrap only on very narrow screens.
Keep line lengths readable and below approximately 65 characters.
Reduce vertical spacing and font sizes gradually rather than radically changing the composition.
Maintain at least 44px interactive tap targets where practical.

requirements: Use semantic header, nav, main, section, and footer elements.
Use aria-current="page" for the active navigation item.
Do not use color alone to communicate state.
